En: There must be another way into the building.
Zh: “肯定有别的办法能进到里面去。”

En: Look, a smoker on the balcony.
Zh: “看，有个人在阳台上抽烟。”

En: This could be our witness.
Zh: “说不定是我们的目击证人。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / KIM BREAKING AND ENTERING barks
En: Make this quick, detective.
Zh: “动作快一点，警探。”

En: You already have the heavy fuel oil to use as paint -- it's red! -- and Cindy the SKULL has a paintbrush. This is on.
Zh: 你已经得到了用作颜料的重燃油——红色的！——而且骷髅头辛迪有一只漆刷。可以开工了。

En: He sounds tired of it all.
Zh: 听起来他已经厌倦了这一切。

En: Your loss. Farewell, rain-washed wall at the end of the world.
Zh: 你的损失。再见，世界尽头这堵被雨水冲刷的墙。

En: Blank, conveying nothing.
Zh: 空的，没有传达任何信息。

En: Why am I looking at this wall?
Zh: 我为什么要看着这堵墙？

En: In the dimming light, some things become clearer.
Zh: 在昏暗的灯光下，有些东西越来越清晰。

En: Dodge ball, really? You don't even have a ball for that.
Zh: 躲避球游戏，真的吗？你连球都没有。

En: You have a keen aesthetic sensibility.
Zh: 你的美学感受性相当敏锐。

En: Cindy's artistic impulses are infectious.
Zh: 辛迪的艺术冲动很有感染力。

En: Dresscode: Pseudo-SKULL.
Zh: 着装要求：伪骷髅头。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You have no clue. It's just a wall.
Zh: 你没有任何线索。它只是一堵墙。

En: Yeah, why? It's a wall, an ordinary wall.
Zh: 是吗，为什么？它是一堵墙，普通的墙。

En: Because -- you see it, finally -- this wall is *sublime*! LOOK AT IT! The shadows, the colours...
Zh: 因为——你总算是看见了——这堵墙代表着*顶点*！看看它！那些阴影，那些色彩…

En: Maybe this would be a good wall for dodge ball?
Zh: 也许这面墙很适合玩躲避球游戏？

En: So many walls all over Martinaise -- weatherworn, cracked, their paint peeling...
Zh: 马丁内斯有那么多面墙——被风雨侵蚀的，残破不堪的，油漆脱落的…

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Why must we stop to look at this wall every time we pass by? We have business to attend to."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“为什么每次路过这堵墙都要停下来看？我们还有别的事要做。”

En: (Let the conceptual joy flow into your pupils and blossom into thoughts in your brain.)
Zh: （让概念上的欢乐流入你的瞳孔，在你的大脑里开花结果。）

En: More!
Zh: 还不够！

En: O WALLFATHER!
Zh: 哦，墙壁之父！

En: Kim! I *must* paint this wall, add even MORE beauty to it.
Zh: “金！我*必须*粉刷这面墙，为它增添更多美丽。”

En: Yes, I must paint this wall, ADD to it! (Get task.)
Zh: 是的，我必须粉刷这面墙，再加点东西！（接受任务。）

En: No. There is nothing to add to perfection. (Back off.)
Zh: 不。这么完美的东西已经没什么好加的了。（离开。）

En: I changed my mind. I *will* paint this wall.
Zh: “我改变主意了。我*会*粉刷这面墙的。”

En: Look the wall over again, carefully. Think about what you could paint there...
Zh: 看着这面墙，再仔细一点。想想你能在上面画什么…

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: This is a wall on the side of an apartment building.
Zh: 这是公寓大楼侧面的一堵墙。

En: Just an ordinary wall. Nothing to see here.
Zh: 只是一堵普通的墙。没什么好看的。

En: The wall waits mutely.
Zh: 墙静静地等待着。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Absolutely. Now you just need the materials.
Zh: 当然了。现在你只要找到材料就行。

En: Huh?
I already have heavy fuel oil, and Cindy the SKULL has a paintbrush...
Zh: 我已经有重燃油了，而且骷髅头辛迪那里有一只漆刷…

En: Cindy the SKULL has what I need.
Zh: 骷髅头辛迪有我需要的东西。

En: I'm sure I'll find them. And then I'll return to this wall.
Zh: 我肯定能找到的。然后再回到墙这里来。

En: I already have the paint. Just need to get a paintbrush from Cindy the SKULL.
Zh: “颜料已经准备好了。只需要从骷髅头辛迪那里拿到漆刷就好。”

En: I must talk to Cindy. Get the necessary *materials* from her.
Zh: “我得跟辛迪聊聊。从她那里拿到必要的*材料*。”

En: I'm sure I'll find the materials. Then we can come back to this magnificent wall.
Zh: “我肯定能找到材料的。到时候我们再回到这堵壮丽的墙边。”

En: He sighs, then adds in a resigned tone: "If you must."
Zh: 他叹了口气，然后用一种听天由命的口吻补充了一句，“如果你非要这么做的话。”

En: I suppose we'll have to pass by it again at some point anyway, he says in a tone of resignation.
Zh: “反正我们之后可能还会路过这个地方，”他的口吻有一种听天由命的味道。

En: Yes, great thinking, go talk to her. The wall will be waiting.
Zh: 是的，这想法不错，去跟她聊聊。这堵墙会一直等着的。

En: Yes, do that. The wall will be waiting.
Zh: 是的，就这么做。这堵墙会一直等着的。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: First, I know you're tired, Kim, but take another look at this wall. Draw *nourishment* from its beauty.
Zh: “首先，我知道你累了，金，但是再看一眼这堵墙。从它的美丽中汲取*营养*。”

En: Mhm, sure. The lieutenant looks up at the wall reluctantly, then back at you.
Zh: “嗯，当然了。”警督不情愿地抬头看了一眼墙，然后又看向你。

En: Your hands shake as they reach for the wall...
Zh: 你的手颤抖地碰触着墙面…

En: The pastels of the wall seem still more luminous in the falling snow.
Zh: 飘落的雪花中，这面墙的色彩似乎更加明亮了。

En: Rain darkens the surface of the most beautiful of all walls, but does not in any way diminish its beauty.
Zh: 雨滴让这幅最美丽的墙面变得昏暗起来，然而它的美丽却没有减弱分毫。

En: There it is -- the most beautiful of walls. Stop and admire it for a moment.
Zh: 这就是——最美丽的墙。在这里驻足片刻，欣赏它的美丽。

En: Because this is no ordinary wall. It is *sublime*! LOOK AT IT! The shadows, the colours...
Zh: 因为这不是一堵普通的墙。它代表着*顶点*！看看它！那些阴影，那些色彩…

En: Cindy the SKULL has a paintbrush and paint. Talk to her.
Zh: 骷髅头辛迪手上有漆刷和颜料。去跟她聊聊吧。

En: You already have the heavy fuel oil to use as paint, and Cindy the SKULL has a paintbrush. This is completely doable.
Zh: 你手上的重燃油可以作为颜料，骷髅头辛迪手上有漆刷。这样完全是可行的。

En: All the other walls on all the other houses must make a pilgrimage in adoration of this, the uncontested pinnacle of wall-craft. Colour peeled from the very face of god.
Zh: 其他房子上的墙一定会专门来这里朝圣，朝拜这个造墙工艺里毫无争议的顶点。只是神明脸上的色彩有些脱落。

En: Your loss. Farewell, light-drenched wall at the end of the world.
Zh: 你的损失。再见，世界尽头这堵沐浴在光芒之下的墙。

En: Your loss. Farewell, snow-flecked wall at the end of the world.
Zh: 你的损失。再见，世界尽头这堵雪花斑驳的墙。

En: Your loss. Farewell, wall at the end of the world, draped in shadow.
Zh: 你的损失。再见，世界尽头这堵笼罩在阴影之下的墙。

En: Cindy the SKULL has all the necessary materials. Talk to her?
Zh: 骷髅头辛迪有所有必要的材料。去找她聊聊？

En: I'll come back to this later. [Leave.]
Zh: “我一会儿再回来。”[离开。]

En: Someone hides behind a curtain: those windows have eyes and those eyes are watching, spying on you three.
Zh: 有人躲在窗帘背后：那些窗户长了眼睛，那些眼睛正在看着你，监视你们三个。

En: Please don't go, I'll even stop smoking inside.
Zh: 请不要走，我甚至可以在心里也把烟戒掉。

En: There has to be a way of getting inside the building -- let's go check out the door near the pier again. Once we've found the way in, we can ask around for his apartment.
Zh: “一定能有办法进去——我们再去码头附近的那扇大门看看。等我们进去之后，可以问问他的房间在哪里。”

En: Why are you whispering? It sounds like you're *already* in trouble.
Zh: “你为什么这么小声？听起来你*已经*遇到麻烦了。”

En: Can you tell me your name?
Zh: “能告诉我你的名字吗？”

En: Son, do you train?
Zh: “小子，你训练过吗？”

En: There's no trouble, I'm just speaking in a *lowered voice*, he says sotto voce.
Zh: “没什么麻烦，我只是声音*很低*而已，”他的声音更低了。

En: Alright, we'll talk later. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我们稍后再聊。”[离开。]

En: Actually, the gendarmerie *really* needs to talk to you.
Zh: “其实吧，我这个宪兵*真的*需要跟你谈谈。”

En: Actually, the police *really* needs to talk to you.
Zh: “其实，警察*真的*需要跟你聊聊。”

En: I don't want to be seen talking to the *gendarmerie*, if that's okay. I just want to finish my cigarette.
Zh: “如果可以的话，我不想别人看见我跟*宪兵队*的人说话。我只想抽完这支烟。”

En: We will remove the body as soon as possible. Now tell us, where were you last Sunday?
Zh: “我们会尽快移走这具尸体。现在请告诉我们，你上周天在哪？”

En: Looks like you've got a good view of the Whirling's backyard. Can you tell me anything about the hanging?
Zh: “你那里似乎能看清飞旋旅社的后院。能不能跟我说说吊人的事？”

En: Oh... He waves lazily with his cigarette. "You already asked me that, didn't you?"
Zh: “哦……”他懒洋洋地摇晃着指尖的香烟。“你已经问过我这个了，不是吗？”

En: Wait, is someone else investigating the lynching?
Zh: 等等，难道还有人在调查私刑的事？

En: Did I?
Zh: “我有吗？”

En: I'm pretty sure I didn't.
Zh: “我很肯定我没有。”

En: No, not you. He gestures idly. "Some more *muscular* type."
Zh: “不，不是你。”他懒散地比了个手势。“是个*肌肉更发达*的家伙。”

En: And when did you speak to this more *muscular* gentleman? The lieutenant takes out his little blue notebook and writes something down.
Zh: 警督拿出蓝色的小笔记本，在上面记了几笔。“你是什么时候跟这位*肌肉*绅士聊天的？”

En: He's deflecting. He's young, with a sharp memory. He hasn't had time to drown it in booze and chemicals.
Zh: 他在转移话题。他很年轻，记忆力很好。他还没时间把它浸泡在酒精和化学药品里。

En: It's the God of Cigarettes and Youth -- ask him if he's got anything to spare.
Zh: 它是香烟和年轻之神——问问他能不能分你点。

En: By god, this young man has the body of a *decathlete*. His lithe form was practically made for vaulting over the high bar!
Zh: 天啦，这个年轻人有一幅*十项全能运动员*的身躯。他那轻盈的身段应该是专门为跃过高杆准备的！

En: There's something about the way he carries himself... Something enviable, mysterious, attractive...
Zh: 他的举止有些说不出的感觉…很让人羡慕，很神秘，很迷人…

En: Hold on... Can I at least have a cigarette?
Zh: “等一下…能分我一根烟吗？”

En: Apologies, but this is my last one. He takes another drag and shivers.
Zh: “抱歉，这是最后一根了。”他又吸了一口，打了个寒颤。

En: No problem. We'll talk more next time. [Leave.]
Zh: “没关系。我们下次再聊。”[离开。]

En: Like a nervous cat, he keeps stealing looks at the neighbouring windows.
Zh: 他就像只紧张的猫，一直偷瞄着邻居家的窗户。

En: I can *tell* that you finally got him down. Thank you -- it was quite a disturbing sight, even by Martinaise standards.
Zh: “我能*看出来*，你总算是把他放下来了。谢谢——就算以马丁内斯的标准来看，这幅景象也实在是有些令人不安。”

En: What were you doing last Sunday?
Zh: “上周日你在做什么？”

En: I'm working on it.
Zh: “我正在努力。”

En: What did you do last Sunday?
Zh: “上周日你在做什么？”

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: You didn't answer the question. What were you doing last Sunday?
Zh: “你还没回答我的问题。上周日你在做什么？”

En: You still haven't answered my question. What were you doing last Sunday?
Zh: “你还没回答我的问题。上周日你在做什么？”

En: He sighs. "I had a friend over."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“我有个朋友来了。”

En: What kind of friend?
Zh: “什么样的朋友？”

En: A *Sunday friend*? How intriguing...
Zh: 一个*周日朋友*？真有趣…

En: Makes sense. Friends are nice on Sunday. You don't have to work, you can just spend time with pals, watching rugby and drinking beer.
Zh: 有道理。周日有朋友陪伴挺好的。不用工作，你可以跟朋友们待在一起，看看橄榄球赛，喝点啤酒。

En: He doesn't reply, gesturing 'no' with his cigarette. Under the dull and darkening sky the neighbouring windows stand silent.
Zh: 他没有回应，用香烟比出了“拒绝”的意思。在阴沉黯淡的夜空下，邻居的窗户也寂静无声。

En: When was that?
Zh: “那是什么时候？”

En: My name? My name is Martin Martinaise.
Zh: “我的名字？我叫马丁·马丁内斯。”

En: That's definitely not his real name.
Zh: 这肯定不是他的真名。

En: You're not actually called Martin Martinaise, are you?
Zh: “马丁·马丁内斯不是你的真名，对吧？”

En: No, of course not. Could you please lower your voice? He scans the courtyard. It's silent like the bottom of a well, every sound captured and reflected back.
Zh: “不，当然不是。你能不能小点声？”他扫视着院子。这里安静的就像井底，每一道声音都会被捕捉到，然后反射回去。

En: Ask him again.
Zh: 再问他一次。

En: Listen, I really need your name.
Zh: “听着，我真的需要知道你的名字。”

En: And I *really* need to finish this cigarette, he replies with a subtle smile.
Zh: “我*真的*需要抽完这根烟，”他回应着，笑容让人难以捉摸。

En: But he hasn't left yet.
Zh: 但是他还没离开。

En: Was it someone from the police?
Zh: “是警察局的人吗？”

En: I couldn't tell you, he says, exhaling a little puff of smoke. "We don't usually see much of the *gendarmerie* around here."
Zh: “我不能告诉你，”他吐出一口烟，说到。“我们这儿*宪兵队*可不常见。”

En: Occasionally. He takes another drag, filling his lungs with nicotine. "Why?"
Zh: “偶尔吧。”他又吸了一口，用尼古丁填满自己的肺部。“为什么这么问？”

En: Are you on the track team?
Zh: “你是田径队的吗？”

En: Nothing. You should give up smoking, son. It's not good for your health.
Zh: “没什么。你应该戒烟，小子。这对你的健康不好。”

En: No, I just like to look good. A light breeze pulls back a flap of shirt.
Zh: “不，我只是想让自己看起来好一点。”微风抚平了衬衫上的一道褶皱。

En: You're right, it's horrible for the skin. Very ageing. He takes another drag, before shaking off the ash.
Zh: “你说得对，而且对皮肤不好。容易变老。”他又吸了一口，随后弹了弹烟灰。

En: A shame. Young man like you, with a body like yours, you'd be a credit to any track team.
Zh: “可惜啊。像你这样的年轻人，就凭你这幅身体，肯定会成为任何田径队的荣耀。”

En: Trust me, I'm *not* a team player, he says with a honeyed smile.
Zh: “相信我，我*不是*适合团队行动的人，”他满脸笑容的说到。

En: No, we won't. He takes one last drag of his cigarette, before stubbing it out on the snowy balcony with a dying hiss.
Zh: “不，不用。”他吞下最后一口香烟，随后把烟蒂扔在雪花覆盖的阳台上，发出一丝垂死的嘶嘶声。

En: No, we won't. He takes one last drag of his cigarette, before stubbing it out on the balcony.
Zh: “不，不会的。”他吞下最后一口香烟，然后把掐灭的烟蒂扔在阳台上。

En: Let him go. [Leave.]
Zh: 让他走吧。[离开。]

En: Convince him to stay.
Zh: 说服他留下来。

En: Workout talk.
Zh: 锻炼的话题。

En: Bumming a cigarette.
Zh: 讨要一根烟。

En: This isn't the place or time for questions: Who knows who might be watching from the distance, hidden behind the curtains.
Zh: 这里不是提问的好地方，也不是好时候：谁知道会不会有人躲在窗帘后面，从远处暗中观察。

En: For a moment the man on the balcony seems almost vulnerable. Something moves in the depths of his feline eyes: compassion, and a hint of understanding. There's snow gathering on his hair and on his shoulders, a speckle of white against the purple that hangs loose on his slender frame.
Zh: 片刻之间，阳台上的男子看起来几乎有些脆弱。他那双猫一样的眼睛深处有什么东西在游移着：怜悯，还有一丝理解。雪花堆积在他的头发和肩膀上，比他那纤细身躯上挂着的衬衫还要更白一些。

En: For a moment the man on the balcony seems almost vulnerable. Something moves in the depths of his feline eyes: compassion, and a hint of understanding.
Zh: 片刻间，阳台上的男孩看起来几乎有些脆弱。他那双猫一样的眼睛深处有什么东西在游移着：怜悯，还有一丝理解。

En: Good luck with the investigation. He walks away.
Zh: “祝你调查顺利。”他走开了。

En: Martin Martinaise? Good local name. Let's go with that.
Zh: 马丁·马丁内斯？本地人叫这名字还真是够应景的。就叫这个吧。

En: Not that he has to worry about getting old yet -- he's sparkling with youth, a radiant demi-god.
Zh: 并不是说他现在就要开始担心变老的问题——他周身散发着闪闪发光的年轻气息，就像个光芒四射的半神。

En: It was my Sunday friend.
Zh: “是我的周日朋友。”

En: He's gone. The lieutenant puts away his notebook and turns to you.
Zh: “他走了。”警督收回笔记本，转向了你。

En: We should run after him, see where he went!
Zh: “我们应该跟上他，看看他去哪了！”

En: No point in running. Tenements like these often have multiple exits. If he doesn't want to talk to us, then he'll know how to hide himself.
Zh: “跑也是无济于事。这种公寓一般都会有好几个出口。如果他不想跟我们说话，肯定知道藏在什么地方。”

En: So we just give up?
Zh: “所以我们就这么放弃吗？”

En: You're right, he probably didn't have anything important to say anyway.
Zh: “你说的对，他可能也没什么重要的话要说。”

En: He could be a witness, him or his *Sunday friend*. Either way we need to look into that *muscular type* who's asking about our case...
Zh: “他可能是个目击证人，他，或者是他那位*周日朋友*。不管怎么说，我们都要调查一下那个*肌肉男*，看看是谁在打探我们的案子…”

En: He could be a witness, him or his *Sunday friend*. It's worth looking into...
Zh: “他可能是个目击证人，他，或者是他那位*周日朋友*。值得调查一下……”

En: He could be a witness. And either way we need to look into that *muscular type* who's asking about our case...
Zh: “他可能是个目击证人，不管怎么说，我们都要调查一下那个*肌肉男*，看看是谁在打探我们的案子…”

En: He could be a witness. We shouldn't rule out the possibility...
Zh: “他可能是个目击证人。我们不应该排除这种可能性……”

En: There has to be a way of getting inside the building. Once we've found the way in, we can ask around for his apartment.
Zh: “一定能有办法进去。等我们进去之后，可以问问他的房间在哪里。”

En: He did leave us a sign, did you see that? He wanted to draw our attention to that stone right over there. The lieutenant nods towards a small rock on a soggy patch of grass.
Zh: “他确实给我们留了个信号，你没看见吗？他想让我们注意那块石头。”警督指向躺在潮湿草地上的一块小石头。

En: Great, let's do that. [Leave.]
Zh: “太好了，就这么干吧。”[离开。]

En: What's your friend's real name? Did he see something?
Zh: “你那位朋友的真名叫什么？他看见什么了吗？”

En: If we find a way inside the building, we can ask around for his apartment.
Zh: “如果我们能想办法进到里面去，就能问出他的房间在哪里。”

En: Time to bring out your secret charm: tears and begging! Show him your *emotional* side, throw yourself before his very feet like a dog!
Zh: 是时候拿出你的秘密法宝了：眼泪和乞求！把你*情绪化*的一面展现给他看，像狗一样扑倒在他的脚边！

En: Is it really that important? he asks you, adjusting his shirt.
Zh: “真有那么重要吗？”他整理了一下身上的衬衫，开口问到。

En: Not looking for any trouble, officer... he says in a quiet voice. Despite the cold, his shirt hangs unbuttoned on his frame.
Zh: “我不想惹什么麻烦，警官…”他小声说到。尽管很冷，但是他身上的衬衫没有扣上。

En: You hiding something? Speak up, this is the police.
Zh: “你在隐瞒什么吗？说出来吧，我们是警察。”

En: It's the voice of someone who has something to *hide* my liege.
Zh: 这种声音就代表某人有什么东西想要*隐藏*，大人。

En: I'm not throwing anything.
Zh: “我什么也不会扔的。”

En: The God Himself has denied you absolution.
Zh: 神明本尊拒绝了你的告解。

En: I get it. Nothing wrong with looking good.
Zh: “我明白。外形好没什么错。”

En: He gives you a honeyed smile, before shaking off the cigarette ash.
Zh: 他露出甜美的微笑，随后弹掉了烟灰。

En: I'd even go so far as to say that the view is a little *too good*, my dear gendarme. Do you have an estimate of when the body will be taken away?
Zh: “我甚至可以说眼前这幅景象有些*太好了*，尊敬的宪兵大人。你估计这具尸体什么时候能被带走？”

En: A downy blanket of white to cover up the miserable poverty of the scene.
Zh: 一张柔软的白毯盖住了这幅悲惨的景象。

En: Did you? (Look at Kim.)
Zh: “是你吗？”（看着金。）

En: No, not you two. He gestures idly. "Some more *muscular* type."
Zh: “不，不是你们俩。”他懒洋洋地靠着。“是个*肌肉发达*的家伙。”

En: Alright, but make it quick. Once I finish this cigarette I have to run.
Zh: “好吧，但是麻烦快一点。等我抽完这根烟就要跑路了。”

En: You see a young man on a balcony nursing a cigarette. His eyes have been following you for a while.
Zh: 你看见一个年轻人倚靠在阳台上，指尖还夹着一支香烟。他盯了你好一会儿了。

En: There won't *be* a next time. He takes a last drag of his cigarette, before stubbing it out in the balcony snow. "I really need to get going."
Zh: “*不会*再有下次。”他吞下最后一口烟，然后把它掐灭在阳台的积雪里。“我真的要走了。”

En: No trouble from me, I just want to know what's going on here.
Zh: “我不想找麻烦，只想知道到底发生了什么事。”

En: Too late, young man. Trouble's found you.
Zh: “太晚了，年轻人。麻烦找上你了。”

En: Sorry, I don't have time for this. I just want to finish my cigarette and be gone.
Zh: “抱歉，我没时间管这个。我只想抽完这根烟，然后离开。”

En: Can't you just like, toss that one down?
Zh: “你就不能把它扔下来吗？”

En: I need absolution!
Zh: “我需要告解！”

En: I'm not sure that's a good idea.
Zh: “我不觉得这是个好主意。”

En: Absolution? I don't know that brand, but I'm pretty sure you can get Astras at the Frittte.
Zh: “告解？我不知道那个牌子，不过我很肯定，你能在弗利多弄到阿斯特拉。”

En: Shut up and throw, kid!
Zh: “闭上你的嘴，快点扔下来，小子！”

En: You're right, that was obviously a bad idea. Forget I said anything.
Zh: “你是对的，这显然是个坏主意。忘了我说的话吧。”

En: There's no way you'd catch that cigarette anyway.
Zh: 这样你是不可能接住香烟的。

En: What, really?
Zh: 什么，真的吗？

En: Yes, really. Don't even try. You'll just embarrass yourself.
Zh: 是的，真的。连试都不要试。你只会让自己蒙羞的。

En: As you wish. He takes another drag.
Zh: “如你所愿。”他又抽了一口。

En: It's a sign!
Zh: 是个信号！

En: Ah, there it goes -- *wasted*! You would have gotten at least a few good drags out of it.
Zh: 啊，看到了吧——*浪费*！你本来还能好好抽上几口的。

En: Last week? I don't know, look... He looks around the courtyard again. Snow blankets the old patio chairs and dead house plants, and all the neighbouring windows are black.
Zh: “上周？我不知道，你看…”他又环视了一下后院。雪花覆盖在破旧的露台椅和枯萎的盆栽上，所有邻居的窗户都是漆黑一片。

En: Last week? I don't know, look... He looks around the courtyard again. Rain patters on the window sills and dead house plants, too late to do them any good.
Zh: “上周？我不知道，你看…”他又环视了一下后院。雨滴拍打在窗台板和枯萎的盆栽上，不过已经太迟，对它们来说没太大用场。

En: Last week? I don't know, look... He looks around the courtyard again. Old patio chairs and dead house plants litter the scene.
Zh: “上周？我不知道，你看…”他又环视了一下后院。破旧的露台椅和枯萎的盆栽散落地到处都是。

En: It'll take more than rain to bring this place back to life.
Zh: 要想让这里重新恢复生机，光凭雨水是远远不够的。

En: He's an actor declaiming a soliloquy. See how you hang on his every word?
Zh: 他是个在唱独白的演员，你没发现他说的每个词都很吸引你吗？

En: He doesn't reply, gesturing 'no' with his cigarette. Under the grey sky the neighbouring windows are streaked with rain.
Zh: 他没有回应，用香烟比出了“拒绝”的意思。灰暗的天空下，邻居的窗户上布满雨点。

En: He doesn't reply, gesturing 'no' with his cigarette. Under the grey sky snow continues to pile on the neighbouring window sills.
Zh: 他没有回应，用香烟比出了“拒绝”的意思。灰暗的天空下，雪花堆积在邻居的窗台板上。

En: He doesn't reply, gesturing 'no' with his cigarette. In the neighbouring windows you can see faint reflections of his silhouette, all from different angles.
Zh: 他没有回应，用香烟比出了“拒绝”的意思。邻居的窗户上隐约映照出他的轮廓，每一个的角度都有所不同。

En: Someone hides behind a curtain: those windows have eyes and those eyes are watching, spying on you two.
Zh: 有人躲在窗帘背后：那些窗户长了眼睛，那些眼睛正在看着你，监视你们俩。

En: Now, if you'll excuse me, I really need to get going.
Zh: “请原谅，但我真的得走了。”

En: Hey, listen... (Lower your voice.) "I am just trying to make things okay again. Can we meet again somewhere else?"
Zh: “喂，听我说…”（压低你的声音。）“我只想让一切都回归正轨。我们能不能换个别的地方见面？”

En: Good luck with the investigation.
Zh: “祝你调查顺利。”

En: I am sorry, but I really don't have the information you're looking for. His voice is soft and deliberate. With a flick of his wrist, he sends the cigarette butt sailing over the rail...
Zh: 他再次开了口，声音柔软而又从容：“很抱歉，但我真的没有你需要的信息。”他挥舞手腕，把烟蒂扔过栏杆…

En: But hold on, what's that? For a split second his hand lingers... as though gesturing towards a stone placed right next to the front door...
Zh: 等一下，那是什么？有那么一瞬间，他的手停留了一下…好像指向了前门旁边的一块石头…

En: Please don't go! I'll stop drinking, I'll... take the trash out, just... please don't leave me.
Zh: “别走！我会戒酒的，我会…把垃圾带出去，只要…别离开我。”

En: What do you want from me? Do you want me to *cry*?! Huh?
Zh: “你想让我怎么样？让我*哭*吗？！哈？”

En: Listen, I really have to go. With a flick of his wrist he sends the extinguished cigarette sailing over the rail.
Zh: “听着，我真的要走了。”他挥舞手腕，把熄灭的烟蒂扔过栏杆…

En: No, for god's sake, I don't want you to cry! He takes a quick step back and looks around, clearly disturbed.
Zh: “没有，看在老天的份上，我可不想你哭啊！”他猛地往后退了一步，看了看四周，显然有些不安。

En: *Trash*? The young man shakes his head in confusion.
Zh: “*垃圾*？”年轻男子困惑的摇摇头。

En: I need to get inside this apartment building. Can you help me?
Zh: “我需要进到公寓里面去。你能帮我吗？”

En: Help you? No, sorry, gendarme... He shakes his charming head. "I have to run."
Zh: “帮你？不，对不起，宪兵先生…”他摇了摇那颗迷人的脑袋。“我得跑路了。”

En: Don't let him go! This could be your witness -- the balcony has a great view of the whole thing.
Zh: 别让他走！他可能是目击证人——那个阳台视野很好，很可能目睹了一切。

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: Of course I've thought about quitting but... for what?
Zh: “我当然想过退出了，但是…为了什么呢？”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: Not everything that needs to be said, needs to be heard.
Zh: “不是所有事都要说出来，都要被听到的。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: Deep down everyone is lonely.
Zh: “每个人内心深处都是孤独的。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: All *real* connections begin in the mind.
Zh: “所有*真正*的关系都是从心里开始的。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: Scary how many people don't have *anyone* to talk to.
Zh: “其实挺吓人的，有那么多人没有可以*倾述的对象*。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks apartment
En: I don't have clients, only friends.
Zh: “我没有客户，只有朋友。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks balcony
En: Hey, what's that noise down there?
Zh: “喂，下面吵吵闹闹的在干什么？”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks balcony
En: It's been a week and now they show up?
Zh: “都过去一星期了，现在他们出现了？”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks balcony
En: I can't get involved in this...
Zh: “我不想被卷进来…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks balcony
En: So much for a quiet smoke.
Zh: “我就想安静的抽根烟。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks balcony
En: I *knew* I should've quit...
Zh: “我就*知道*我应该退出…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: *How* can a bar be out of vermouth?
Zh: “酒吧*怎么会*没有苦艾酒呢？”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Can't do it with class? Don't do it at all.
Zh: “不能有风度地做这件事吗？那就干脆别做了。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Could I get a refill here, please?
Zh: “麻烦，我这里再来一杯？”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Don't even get me started on beauty...
Zh: “不要在我面前挑起美的话题…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: I'm practically a shrink minus the diploma.
Zh: “我基本就是个没文凭的心理医生。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Yes, yes, I'm very attractive. Great, thank you.
Zh: “是啊，是啊。我是很有吸引力。很好，谢谢你。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Okay, *you* tell me this doesn't taste like shit...
Zh: “好吧，*你*告诉我这玩意的味道没那么糟糕…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: *Obviously* I'm gonna quit some day.
Zh: “*显然*我总有一天会退出。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: *How* can a bar be out of vermouth?
Zh: “酒吧*怎么会*没有苦艾酒呢？”

En: Can't do it with class? Don't do it at all.
Zh: “不能有风度地做这件事吗？那就干脆别做了。”

Zh: “麻烦，我这里再来一杯？”

En: Yes, yes, I'm very attractive. Great, thank you.
Zh: “是啊，是啊。我是很有吸引力。很好，谢谢你。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: *Obviously* I'm gonna quit some day.
Zh: “*显然*我总有一天会退出。”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Okay, *you* tell me this doesn't taste like shit...
Zh: “好吧，*你*告诉我这玩意的味道没那么糟糕…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: Don't even get me started on beauty...
Zh: “不要在我面前挑起美的话题…”

ConvTitle: BACKYARD / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY barks whirling
En: I'm practically a shrink minus the diploma.
Zh: “我基本就是个没文凭的心理医生。”

En: A stone like any other, lying in a whorl of sleet and mud. Maybe there's something under it?
Zh: 很普通的石头，躺在冻雨和泥泞的漩涡里。也许下面有什么东西？

En: Turn it over.
Zh: 把它翻过来。

En: Ignore it. [Leave.]
Zh: 无视它。[离开。]

En: There's a key beneath it, rusty from the dirt.
Zh: 下面有一把钥匙，因为尘土的腐蚀有些生锈。

En: Put the stone back. [Leave.]
Zh: 把石头放回去。[离开。]

En: This must be for the front door. Pity it doesn't have the apartment number on it. This building has many apartments and the man can be in any of them...
Zh: “这肯定是前门的钥匙。可惜上面没有公寓号码。这栋建筑里有很多套公寓，那个人可能住在其中任意一间…”

En: Looks like it could belong to the building's front door.
Zh: 看来是建筑前门的钥匙。

En: How are we going to find the right one?
Zh: “我们怎么找到正确的那一个呢？”

En: This must be for the front door. Let's see if it works.
Zh: “这肯定是前门的钥匙。我们试试能不能用吧。”

En: We'll just have to go in and see.
Zh: “我们得进去看了。”

En: A stone like any other, lying in a whorl of sleet and mud. Maybe there's something under it?
Zh: 很普通的石头，躺在冻雨和泥泞的漩涡里。也许下面有什么东西？

En: Turn it over.
Zh: 把它翻过来。

En: Ignore it. [Leave.]
Zh: 无视它。[离开。]

Zh: 下面有一把钥匙，因为尘土的腐蚀有些生锈。

En: Put the stone back. [Leave.]
Zh: 把石头放回去。[离开。]

Zh: “这肯定是前门的钥匙。可惜上面没有公寓号码。这栋建筑有上百套公寓。”

En: Looks like it could belong to the building's front door.
Zh: 看来是建筑前门的钥匙。

En: How are we going to find the right one?
Zh: “我们怎么找到正确的那一个呢？”

En: This must be for the front door. Let's see if it works.
Zh: “这肯定是前门的钥匙。我们试试能不能用吧。”

Zh: “我们得进去看了。”
